question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getMaxStreamResponseSize()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the unit ambiguity in @DataSizeUnit(DataUnit.MEGABYTES) by injecting a value with an explicit byte suffix (e.g., '36B') that overrides the default unit annotation, setting maxStreamResponseSize to 36 bytes and causing all gRPC block streaming to fail with message-too-large errors, resulting in a denial-of-service?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getMaxSubscribeAttempts()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the errors AtomicInteger in BlockNode is reset to 0 after reaching maxSubscribeAttempts, combined with a maxSubscribeAttempts of 1, to create a scenario where a malicious block node alternates between one success and one failure, keeping the error counter perpetually at 0 and never triggering the circuit-breaker?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getReadmitDelay()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the readmitDelay by injecting the minimum value of 10 seconds, combined with a scenario where all block nodes fail simultaneously, to create a 10-second window of complete block streaming unavailability that repeats every 10 seconds, causing data gaps in the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getResponseTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the responseTimeout of 400ms by controlling a block node that deliberately responds to ServerStatusRequest in exactly 399ms, staying just within the deadline while consuming maximum CPU/thread resources on the importer side for each status check?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getMaxStreamResponseSize()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a minimum constraint on maxStreamResponseSize (only @NotNull) by injecting DataSize.ofBytes(1), causing the gRPC channel to reject all block streaming responses as too large, effectively disabling block ingestion without triggering any validation error?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getMaxBlockItems()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the int arithmetic in BlockAssembler.append() where `pendingCount += blockItems.size()` can overflow if blockItems.size() is large and pendingCount is near Integer.MAX_VALUE, causing pendingCount to wrap to a negative value that is always less than maxBlockItems, bypassing the overflow protection?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getMaxStreamResponseSize()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Spring Boot relaxed binding for DataSize properties by injecting a malformed string (e.g., 'NaN' or 'infinity') as the maxStreamResponseSize value, causing a DataSize parsing exception at startup that prevents the importer from starting, resulting in a denial-of-service?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getReadmitDelay()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the readmitDelay by injecting a malformed ISO-8601 Duration string (e.g., 'PT-0.001S') that passes the @DurationMin(seconds = 10) check due to parsing edge cases, causing the readmitTime to be set incorrectly and disrupting the circuit-breaker timing logic?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getResponseTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the responseTimeout by injecting a Duration with nanosecond precision (e.g., PT0.0000001S) that rounds down to 0 milliseconds when converted, causing withDeadlineAfter(0, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS) to set an immediately-expired deadline and fail all getBlockRange() calls?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getMaxSubscribeAttempts()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the race condition between errors.incrementAndGet() and errors.set(0) in BlockNode.onError() by triggering concurrent errors from multiple threads, causing the error count to exceed maxSubscribeAttempts multiple times before the reset, leading to multiple readmitTime updates and an unexpectedly long cooldown period?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getMaxStreamResponseSize()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that maxStreamResponseSize is applied per-channel (set at channel construction time in BlockNode constructor) and not dynamically updated, meaning that if the configuration is changed at runtime to a smaller value, existing channels retain the old (larger) limit, allowing oversized messages to bypass the new restriction?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getMaxBlockItems()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that maxBlockItems is checked only in BlockAssembler.append() after items are already added to the pending list, meaning that a malicious block node can cause the importer to allocate memory for up to maxBlockItems+N items before the exception is thrown and the list is discarded?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getReadmitDelay()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the readmitDelay by injecting a value of exactly Duration.ofSeconds(10) (the minimum), combined with a maxSubscribeAttempts of 1, to create a scenario where a single network hiccup causes a 10-second block streaming gap, and repeated hiccups cause cascading 10-second gaps that accumulate into significant data lag?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getResponseTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the responseTimeout being used only for getBlockRange() (ServerStatusRequest) but not for the main streamBlocks() call, meaning that a malicious block node can accept the subscription and then send responses arbitrarily slowly, holding the streaming thread indefinitely without triggering the responseTimeout?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getMaxStreamResponseSize()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Lombok @Data annotation on StreamProperties (which generates public setters) to inject a crafted DataSize value at runtime through any Spring-managed bean that has access to the StreamProperties instance, bypassing the @Validated constraint that only runs at construction/binding time?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getMaxBlockItems()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Lombok @Data-generated setMaxBlockItems() setter to inject a value below the @Min(1000) constraint at runtime (after Spring validation has already passed), causing the BlockAssembler to throw BlockStreamException for any block with more than the injected value of items?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getMaxSubscribeAttempts()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Lombok @Data-generated setMaxSubscribeAttempts() setter to inject 0 at runtime (bypassing the @Min(1) constraint), causing the `errors.incrementAndGet() >= 0` check to always be true and marking every block node inactive on the very first connection attempt?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getReadmitDelay()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Lombok @Data-generated setReadmitDelay() setter to inject null at runtime (bypassing the @NotNull constraint), causing a NullPointerException when Instant.now().plus(null) is called in BlockNode.onError(), crashing the block streaming thread?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getResponseTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Lombok @Data-generated setResponseTimeout() setter to inject null at runtime (bypassing the @NotNull constraint), causing a NullPointerException when blockNodeService.withDeadlineAfter(null) is called in BlockNode.getBlockRange(), crashing the block range discovery?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getMaxStreamResponseSize()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Spring Boot environment variable binding (e.g., HIERO_MIRROR_IMPORTER_BLOCK_STREAM_MAXSTREAMRESPONSESIZE) in a containerized deployment to override maxStreamResponseSize to a value that causes integer overflow in the BlockNode constructor cast, without requiring any file system access?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getMaxBlockItems()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Spring Boot environment variable binding (e.g., HIERO_MIRROR_IMPORTER_BLOCK_STREAM_MAXBLOCKITEMS) to set maxBlockItems to exactly 1000 (the minimum), making the importer reject any block with more than 1000 items as a BlockStreamException, causing all modern blocks (which likely exceed 1000 items) to fail processing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getMaxSubscribeAttempts()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Spring Boot environment variable binding (e.g., HIERO_MIRROR_IMPORTER_BLOCK_STREAM_MAXSUBSCRIBEATTEMPTS) to set maxSubscribeAttempts to 1, combined with a network environment where transient errors are common, causing all block nodes to be continuously cycled through inactive states and preventing stable block streaming?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getReadmitDelay()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Spring Boot environment variable binding (e.g., HIERO_MIRROR_IMPORTER_BLOCK_STREAM_READMITDELAY) to set readmitDelay to the maximum representable Duration, causing any block node that hits the error threshold to be permanently deactivated with a readmitTime that will never be reached in practice?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getResponseTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Spring Boot environment variable binding (e.g., HIERO_MIRROR_IMPORTER_BLOCK_STREAM_RESPONSETIMEOUT) to set responseTimeout to 100ms (the minimum), causing the withDeadlineAfter() deadline to expire on any block node with more than 100ms round-trip latency, marking all geographically distant nodes as unreachable?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getMaxStreamResponseSize()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that maxStreamResponseSize is set at channel construction time and channels are only recreated when BlockNodeProperties change (not when StreamProperties change), meaning a runtime update to maxStreamResponseSize has no effect on existing channels, creating a security gap where the old (potentially unsafe) size limit persists?
